schools.summary: Gill: Moses went up from the plains of Moab, where he had repeated the law, sung the song, and blessed the tribes, at the command of God; the LORD showed him all the land.

themes: Moses went up... unto the mountain of Nebo... And the LORD shewed him all the land
God showing the dying servant the land
seen the promises afar off (Heb. 11:13)

schools.summary: Gill: Naphtali, in the north, where was Galilee of the Gentiles, most frequented by the Messiah when come.
Keil & Delitzsch: the lands of Ephraim, Manasseh, and Judah unto the utmost sea.

themes: And all Naphtali, and the land of Ephraim, and Manasseh, and all the land of Judah, unto the utmost sea
the whole land of promise shown
Galilee of the Gentiles, where the Messiah would walk

schools.summary: Gill: the southern part of the land, all of it, then the view directed eastward.
Ellicott: the south (Negeb), the plain of Jordan, and the valley of Jericho, the city of palm trees.

themes: And the south, and the plain of the valley of Jericho, the city of palm trees
the whole land surveyed to the south
the gateway of the conquest shown

schools.summary: Gill: the Word of the Lord, as the Jerusalem Targum, having shown him all the land.
Ellicott: the oath to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob now shown fulfilled to Moses' eyes.

themes: This is the land which I sware unto Abraham... I have caused thee to see it... but thou shalt not go over
God's faithfulness and his sentence together
the law sees the rest, yet brings not in (Heb. 4:8)

schools.summary: Gill: he died on a mountain in Moab, contrary to some Jewish writers who deny he died; he died according to the word of the LORD, honoured as the servant of the LORD.

themes: So Moses the servant of the LORD died there in the land of Moab, according to the word of the LORD
the crowning title, servant of the LORD
well done, thou good and faithful servant (Matt. 25:21)

schools.summary: Gill: Jarchi and other Jewish writers say the Lord buried him, perhaps by angels; though he died on the mount, he was buried in a valley; no man knows his sepulchre.

themes: he buried him in a valley... but no man knoweth of his sepulchre unto this day
God himself burying his servant in a hidden grave
Michael disputed about the body of Moses (Jude 9)

schools.summary: Gill: his age divided into three equal periods — forty years in Pharaoh's court, forty in Midian, forty in the government of Israel; his eye not dim nor his force abated.

themes: Moses was an hundred and twenty years old when he died: his eye was not dim, nor his natural force abated
God sustaining his servant in full vigour
fruitful in old age, fat and flourishing (Ps. 92:14)

schools.summary: Gill, with Josephus and the Samaritan Chronicle: they wept vehemently when he signified his death and took his leave, and mourned publicly thirty days.

themes: the children of Israel wept for Moses in the plains of Moab thirty days
a fitting tribute of grief to their leader
great lamentation made over the faithful (Acts 8:2)

schools.summary: Gill: Joshua was abundantly qualified by the spirit of wisdom for the government of Israel, a type of Christ, on whom the spirit of wisdom rests.
Henry: the succession and the people hearkening to him.

themes: Joshua the son of Nun was full of the spirit of wisdom... and the children of Israel hearkened unto him
God's care continuing beyond the leader
Joshua a type of Christ, the spirit of wisdom on him (Isa. 11:2)

schools.summary: Gill: there arose not a prophet like Moses in all the Old Testament — not Joshua, Samuel, or any to the time of Ezra; the LORD knew him face to face.

themes: there arose not a prophet since in Israel like unto Moses, whom the LORD knew face to face
Moses unequalled among the prophets of the law
Moses a servant, but Christ a son (Heb. 3:5-6)

schools.summary: Gill: the Targums read 'which the Word of the Lord sent him to do' — he who appeared in the bush and sent him to Egypt to work miracles, to Pharaoh, his servants, and his land.

themes: In all the signs and the wonders, which the LORD sent him to do in the land of Egypt
the works confirming Moses' singular commission
a shadow of the greater works of Christ (John 15:24)

schools.summary: Gill: the mighty hand, in all done by his hand — stretched over the sea to divide it, smiting the rocks; and the great terror shown in the sight of all Israel; this closes the book.

themes: And in all that mighty hand, and in all the great terror which Moses shewed in the sight of all Israel
the Pentateuch sealed with Moses' unequalled greatness
the law by Moses, grace and truth by Christ (John 1:17)
